The IDE failed to install or update some plugins. Please try again, and if the problem persists, report it to the support.

The cause: java.nio.file.AccessDeniedException: path_to_installation_dir\config\plugins\github-copilot-intellij\copilot-agent\native\win32-x64\copilot-language-server.exe

So I stay with version V1.12.1

  6. Idea is slowing down because of this plugin, despite it does not work.

comment:
The GitHub Copilot IntelliJ plugin current state is unacceptable for serious WSL-based development. When opening projects through IntelliJ WSL integration (UNC path \wsl.loclahost), the agent frequently fails to read or write workspace files.

Known issues around UNC paths, project root resolution, AGENTS.md, skills, and workspace prompts remain unresolved and even unprioritized by feedback team, see

  • https://github.com/microsoft/copilot-intellij-feedback/issues/1682
  • https://github.com/microsoft/copilot-intellij-feedback/issues/1804

the result is a broken developer experience where Copilot loses context, ignores provided files, or tries unnecessary workarounds.

I mean supporting WSL is not an edge case, it's just how many developers work.

The plugin also lacks important capabilities available in the Copilot CLI, such as configuring model thinking effort, but that does not seem important to ms ? https://github.com/microsoft/copilot-intellij-feedback/issues/1837

my 2 cents : after extensive usage, the IntelliJ plugin feels far behind competing tools like Claude Code in reliability, agent behavior, and overall usefullness.

Workarounds such as mapping WSL paths to network drives should not be required for a premium developer product. It feels like the team is following a roadmap instead of addressing repeated user feedback and blocking bugs. Sorry for being harsh, but for a multi-billion-dollar company, the current quality and responsiveness of this plugin are simply not acceptable.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
Avoid at all cost!

This "integration" is the utter most garbage there is. Sloppily created (most likely AI slop), full of bugs, and far behind the CLI version or any other integration for other IDEs.

Does not respect Planning Mode; it will code anyway. Does not respect file system boundaries; it will access everything. Does not work when executing something on a terminal; will say 'program did not answer' while it's still running, and it will not see the terminal's output. Does not respect settings; will choose default garbage GPT model.

DO NOT USE.
